Summary of Contents: 
This guideline covers assessing the familial and genetic risk of 
having a pathogenic variant associated with ovarian cancer in 
adults.

and genetic risk of 
having a pathogenic variant associated with ovarian cancer in 
adults.

within 12 months of endorsement.

Dear Colleagues 
NICE Clinical Guideline NG241 - Ovarian cancer: identifying and managing familial and 
genetic risk - https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng241 
 
The Department has recently reviewed the above NICE guidance and has formally endorsed 
it as applicable in Northern Ireland.

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
the above NICE guidance and has formally endorsed 
it as applicable in Northern Ireland.   
 
It is important to draw your attention to Recommendations 1.4.5 and 1.5 - Gene panel 
testing - due to current differences in testing methodologies across the UK, health 
care practitioners should seek advice from the Regional Genetics Service in Belfast 
City Hospital. 
 
In accordance with the process outlined in circular HSC (SQSD) 13/22, (https://www.health-
ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/health/doh-hsc-sqsd-13-22.pdf) the following actions 
should be taken:

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
/files/publications/health/doh-hsc-sqsd-13-22.pdf) the following actions 
should be taken: 
 
1. PHA  
a. In conjunction with the Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) 
Department of Health, identify Commissioning and Professional Leads who will 
consider the commissioning implications of the Clinical Guideline and co-
ordinate with any other relevant commissioning teams. The Lead / Leads will 
identify any areas where regional planning / investment / commissioning are 
required, or where there is material risk to safety or quality.

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
/ investment / commissioning are 
required, or where there is material risk to safety or quality. These will then be 
actioned immediately through normal commissioning arrangements or through 
bespoke arrangements reflecting the nature of the issue / risk.   
 
2. HSC Trusts  
a. Proceed with targeted dissemination, agree a Clinical / Management Lead to 
coordinate implementation and consider what has to be done to achieve 
implementation using a risk based assessment and baseline review as 
appropriate to support planning. These initial actions should be undertaken 
within a 3 month period.

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
to support planning. These initial actions should be undertaken 
within a 3 month period. 
 
b. Implement the Guideline within a further 9 months (apart from any elements 
where significant issues have been raised with the SPPG). 
 
c. Provide positive assurances to the SPPG that the required initial actions have 
been taken within the 3 month planning period and that the Guideline has been 
implemented within a further 9 months, where appropriate.

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
and that the Guideline has been 
implemented within a further 9 months, where appropriate. 
 
d. Where significant investment / commissioning needs cannot be met within the 
usual timeframe, notify the SPPG at the earliest opportunity through the routine 
Director-level service issues and performance meetings and agree appropriate 
arrangements with them to achieve implementation.

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
performance meetings and agree appropriate 
arrangements with them to achieve implementation. 
 
e. It is also recognised that it may not be appropriate for a HSC Trust to 
implement every single recommendation. A risk based assessment can be 
used to ensure that all significant safety and quality improvements are 
achieved.   
 
f. Where NICE indicate that a recommendation is critical, in terms of service 
users’ safety and / or outcomes, the Department expects HSC Trusts to 
implement the guidance.  However, this does not override the responsibility of

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
Trusts to 
implement the guidance.  However, this does not override the responsibility of 
 
 
  
clinicians to make decisions appropriate to the circumstances of an individual 
service user. These decisions should be taken in consultation with the 
individual (or their family/ carer/ guardian). 
 
 
3. RQIA 
a. Disseminate the Guideline to the independent sector as appropriate. 
 
4. HSC Special Agencies and NDPBs 
a. Take account of this Guideline in training and other developments as 
appropriate.

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
and NDPBs 
a. Take account of this Guideline in training and other developments as 
appropriate. 
 
To inform the planning process, please find attached details from the Departmental review. 
You should consider and take account of other relevant Departmental policies and strategies 
in your planning and implementation of this guideline, as well as any legislative / policy 
caveats identified in the course of the Departmental review.

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
well as any legislative / policy 
caveats identified in the course of the Departmental review. 
 
A full current list of NICE guidance endorsed for application in Northern Ireland can be found 
on the Department’s website at https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/topics/safety-and-quality-
standards/national-institute-health-and-care-excellence-nice 
 
With thanks for your work in implementing this NICE Clinical Guideline – NG241. 
 
 
 
Professor Cathy Harrison 
Chief Pharmaceutical Officer

Endorsed NICE guidance - Details from Departmental review

Officer  
 

 
 
  
Appendix 1 
Endorsed NICE guidance - Details from Departmental review 
 
Reference Number NICE Clinical Guideline – NG241 
https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng241 
Title  
 
Ovarian cancer: identifying and managing familial and 
genetic risk 
Summary of 
guidance 
This guideline covers assessing the familial and genetic 
risk of having a pathogenic variant associated with 
ovarian cancer in adults.

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
and genetic 
risk of having a pathogenic variant associated with 
ovarian cancer in adults. 
The guideline includes recommendations on: 
➢ organisation of services 
➢ information and support 
➢ assessing the risk of having a pathogenic variant 
➢ criteria for genetic counselling and genetic testing 
➢ assessing the risk of developing ovarian cancer 
➢ primary preventive medicines 
➢ risk-reducing surgery 
NICE has also produced Clinical Guideline: 
➢ CG122 - Ovarian cancer:

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
➢ risk-reducing surgery 
NICE has also produced Clinical Guideline: 
➢ CG122 - Ovarian cancer: recognition and initial 
management (endorsed by DoH in May 2012) - 
https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/cg122 
Related strategically 
relevant DoH/ HSC 
policies 
A Cancer Strategy for Northern Ireland 2022-2032 - 
https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/publications/cancer-strategy-
northern-ireland-2022-2032

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
-2032 - 
https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/publications/cancer-strategy-
northern-ireland-2022-2032 
 
Inter-Departmental 
interest 
None 
Legislative / policy 
caveats 
This advice does not override or replace the individual 
responsibility of health professionals to make appropriate 
decisions in the circumstances of their individual patients, 
in consultation with the patient and/or guardian or carer.

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
of their individual patients, 
in consultation with the patient and/or guardian or carer. 
This would, for example, include situations where 
individual patients have other conditions or complications 
that need to be taken into account in determining whether 
the NICE guidance is fully appropriate in their case. 
It should be noted that this guidance contains some 
recommendations for off-label use of medicines. Trusts 
and practitioners must be aware of their responsibilities 
and ensure that appropriate polices are in place when 
medicines are used off-label.

document_name: Www.health Ni.gov.uk Circular Hsc Sqsd Nice Ng241 13 24
text:
and ensure that appropriate polices are in place when 
medicines are used off-label. 
This guidance makes reference to NICE Social Care 
guidance which has not been endorsed by the DoH.
